growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Lactobacillus reuteri I49 (Firmicutes), Max OD 0.62, Mean μ ± SD 0.45 ± 0.04.
growth_records.notes: μ=0.45 h^-1 (±0.04) reported. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.45 = 1.540 h. Max OD600 = 0.62. Separate 10 mM supplementation experiment: malate 'almost doubled' and fumarate had 'an even more dramatic effect' on L. reuteri growth rate (Fig 4A) — exact supplemented μ values not in text (in figure only), so recorded here only as the base mBHI condition.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 135238 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/135238

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 135238 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/135238

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
